title: Port remaining advanced movement physics details

Fill in the remaining ground detection, steps, collision nuance, and movement-feel details that still diverge from C.

title: Port runtime particle parity beyond opaque-side staging
description:
Align runtime particle staging/blending with the final renderer ordering instead of resolving through the opaque side.

title: Polish hostname and player-setup editing UX
description:
Finish hostname editing and related player/multiplayer setup details.

title: Add remaining HUD special cases and score overlays
description:
Finish score/deathmatch overlays and other remaining special-case HUD behavior.

title: Align save-file search behavior with C load/save UX

title: Finish command-loop accumulation parity
description:
Align command accumulation/timing to support more faithful movement and prediction.
Sources: docs/QUICK_REFERENCE.txt:73-76; docs/PORT_PARITY_TODO.md sections 2 and 10

title: Decide and document gogpu's support target
description:
Make an explicit project decision about whether gogpu is experimental, fallback-only, or expected to reach a bounded parity target.
Sources: docs/PORT_PARITY_TODO.md:13-33,376-394; docs/PORT_PARITY_REVIEW.md:7,16,32

title: Extend music support beyond WAV-backed CD tracks
description:
Add broader codec/format behavior needed to match Ironwail/Quake expectations more closely.
Sources: docs/PORT_PARITY_REVIEW.md:18,34; docs/QUICK_REFERENCE.txt:48-54,200-203

title: Improve prediction, movement, and demo fidelity
description:
Prediction, bounded physics, and forward demo support are present, but advanced movement feel and some client-state polish still diverge. Tighten runtime feel and tooling without reopening already-landed baseline work.
Sources: docs/PARITY_ANALYSIS_INDEX.md:84; docs/PORT_PARITY_REVIEW.md:17,20,33; docs/PORT_PARITY_TODO.md:34-67; docs/QUICK_REFERENCE.txt:66-76,112-115

title: Add deathmatch scoreboard and multiplayer HUD polish

title: Add timedemo and rewind tooling parity
description:
Add timedemo/rewind support after forward-path runtime parity is stable.
Sources: docs/PORT_PARITY_REVIEW.md:20; docs/QUICK_REFERENCE.txt:75-76

title: Reassess older non-parity TODO signals before promoting them to active backlog
description:
Re-check older secondary TODO signals like triangle-path/entity-rendering/lightmap notes before turning them into active implementation issues.
Sources: docs/QUICK_REFERENCE.txt:121-125,189-220; internal/renderer/renderer_gogpu.go:1067-1072; internal/renderer/world.go:1-2

title: Finish save/load UX and transition parity
description:
Save/load data handling is already strong, so the remaining work is mostly UX and transition fidelity. Align save-file discovery, menu entry points, and loading-plaque behavior with the C engine.
Sources: docs/PARITY_ANALYSIS_INDEX.md:81-82; docs/PORT_PARITY_REVIEW.md:19,36; docs/PORT_PARITY_TODO.md:284-314; docs/QUICK_REFERENCE.txt:78-83,101-105

title: Implement remote reconnect and loading-plaque UX parity

title: If retained, triage gogpu world/lightmap/depth-texture gaps
description:
Track gogpu-specific world rendering gaps like lightmap/depth-texture follow-up separately from canonical parity acceptance.
Sources: internal/renderer/world.go:1-2; docs/PORT_PARITY_TODO.md:376-394

title: Revalidate older parity-support docs and secondary TODO signals
description:
Older support docs still contain outdated claims relative to the active parity review/todo set. Use this epic to track housekeeping that prevents future planning from regressing to stale assumptions.
Sources: docs/PARITY_ANALYSIS_INDEX.md:73-86; docs/PARITY_SUMMARY.md:1-16; docs/parity_report.md:1-35

title: Reconcile stale completion and missing-renderer claims in older support docs
description:
Update or annotate older docs that still describe much lower completion and missing renderer basics.
Sources: docs/PARITY_SUMMARY.md:4-16; docs/parity_report.md:29-35; docs/PORT_PARITY_REVIEW.md:11-20,30-37

title: Revalidate and fix external skybox edge cases
description:
Audit remaining external/per-face skybox fallback and scene-order edge cases, then fix any parity mismatches.

title: Decide and document gogpu backend scope after OpenGL parity
description:
Current docs explicitly treat gogpu as secondary and warn against letting it block parity. Clarify gogpu scope first, then do bounded catch-up only if intentionally chosen.
Sources: docs/PARITY_ANALYSIS_INDEX.md:83; docs/PORT_PARITY_REVIEW.md:7,16,32; docs/PORT_PARITY_TODO.md:5-10,376-394

title: Complete remote networking and multiplayer parity
description:
Local loopback flows are substantially wired, but remote transport-backed connect/reconnect and broader multiplayer UX remain incomplete. Finish the work that keeps the port effectively local-only.
Sources: docs/PARITY_ANALYSIS_INDEX.md:80-82; docs/PORT_PARITY_REVIEW.md:30,33,35-37; docs/PORT_PARITY_TODO.md:344-375; docs/QUICK_REFERENCE.txt:56-64,95-100

title: Revalidate inventory and gameplay-state restoration coverage
description:
Add focused verification for inventory/gameplay-state restoration parity.
Sources: docs/QUICK_REFERENCE.txt:78-83

title: Finalize OpenGL pass ordering and viewmodel placement
description:
Bring top-level OpenGL frame sequencing the rest of the way to C behavior, especially final translucency ordering and late viewmodel placement.
Sources: docs/PORT_PARITY_TODO.md:276-277; docs/QUICK_REFERENCE.txt:29-35,89-93
close_reason: Verified the OpenGL pass-order/viewmodel-placement slice is already implemented; refreshed parity tracking docs.

title: Make all menu entry points use the canonical save/load path
description:
Ensure every relevant menu/UI route reaches the same save/load implementation and failure behavior.

title: Unify load/reconnect plaque behavior across local and remote transitions

title: If retained, implement gogpu entity-rendering baseline

gogpu still has an explicit entity-rendering stub; only schedule this after the scope decision and after major OpenGL blockers are largely resolved.

title: Finish authoritative OpenGL render-fidelity parity
description:
OpenGL is the acceptance baseline, and remaining gaps are mostly render-fidelity/integration differences. Finish pass ordering, sprite, skybox, particles, and water-safety parity on the canonical renderer.

title: Polish menus, HUD, config behavior, and runtime UX

Core menu, HUD, console, and config systems are live, but several high-visibility polish items remain. Focus on player-facing gaps that are smaller than the networking/rendering blockers but still important for parity.

title: Expand audio/music fidelity beyond current baseline

title: Revalidate QUICK_REFERENCE submenu and physics bullets against canonical review
